observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
The Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary correcting entries in the year-end financial statements;
The Municipal Treasurer to strictly require the remittance of collections from the RCC to the Liquidating Officer at the close of each day, and to observe timely the submission of the Report of Collections and Deposits to the Municipal Accountant; and
The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to exert utmost effort to retrieve files that will show details of accountability of the balance of ₱116,039.82, and/or make an appropriate accounting entry if found to be non-existent.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:
The Inventory Committee to complete the physical count of the PPE items which should include all the movable properties and immovable properties owned by the LGU such as Land, Buildings and Infrastructure Assets, and submit to the Audit Team and the Accounting Office the corresponding Report on Physical Count of Immovable PPE.
The Inventory Committee and the Accounting Office reconcile their records based on the results of the physical inventory to arrive at the reconciled balances of the PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:
Immediately make the necessary reconciliation of balances of Due to BIR and Due to GSIS accounts and effect the adjustments in the financial statements; and
Consider the preparation and maintenance of individual subsidiary ledgers for all suppliers and employees to ensure accurate and reliable monthly withholding and remittance of taxes, contributions, and loan amortizations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to be guided with the descriptions of the accounts provided under COA Circular No. 2015-009, so that the correct accounts will be used, and to prevent the misstatement of each account.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to:
Comply with the disclosure requirements of the pertinent IPSAS; and
Provide other information relevant to other balances in the FS that are required to be disclosed by the applicable guidelines.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the monthly trial balance and quarterly financial statements together with the supporting schedules. Likewise, strategize on how to submit on or before the 14th day of February of each year, the annual financial statements together with the schedules and subsidiary ledgers, otherwise consider the imposition of the penalty of withholding the salary of the person responsible for the delay as mandated in PD 1445.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly require all the Revenue Collectors to turn-over daily and intact, all collections in accordance with Sections 26 and 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382; and
Require the Municipal Treasurer/Liquidating Officer to deposit intact and within the prescribed period all collections including those turned over to him by the Collectors.

We commended the Municipal Accountant for its timely remittances of loan repayments and contributions to Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth, and recommend to continuously comply with the reglementary period for the remittance of amounts withheld from employees.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the HRMO-Designate to:
Advise the immediate supervisors to require their Job Order workers/employees to reflect in the accomplishment report the actual and quantified accomplishments delivered and/or services rendered during the 8-hour work day; and
Require the immediate supervisors to make a work schedule acknowledged by the job order personnel concerned and/or enroll the JO workers in the biometric system of the LGU based on the agreed schedule to record their actual attendance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to institute necessary measures to minimize instances of frequent cancellation of checks during check preparation for the disbursement vouchers in order to avoid wastage of government resources.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning & Development Officer, the Municipal Engineer, the Bids and Awards Committee, and other officials concerned, to coordinate with each other to fast track and monitor the completion of the development projects. Moreover, we recommended the realignment of those projects that are not implementable to PPA/s that would immediately benefit the people.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the LDRRMO to fast track the implementation of priority PPAs out of the DRRM funds and henceforth implement all LDRMM –PPAS as programmed in the AIP and LDRRMF Investment Plan in order to enhance the LGU’s pre-disaster and post-disaster capabilities.

We recommended that the Local School Board:
Furnish the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and the Municipal Budget Officer with copies of the annual SEF Budget not later than seven days after its approval; and
Submit the quarterly and annual SEF utilization reports to the DepEd Central Office, through appropriate channels, with a copy furnished each to the Sangguniang Bayan of Municipality of Biri, Northern Samar and regional offices of the DBM and DILG.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare a correcting entry and facilitate the return of transferred funds for DRRM-Capital Outlay PPAs back to the General Fund, in the absence of LDRRMC Resolution that the said funds are to be re-appropriated for other purpose. Henceforth, ensure that only the unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRM-MOOE is transferred to the Special Trust Fund at the end of each year.
observations.managementAction: Necessary correcting entries were already made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office to formulate policies and guidelines on the grant of financial and burial assistance, ratified by the Sangguniang Bayan, to ensure the validity and propriety of the disbursements made in providing assistance to clients/recipients as well as to ensure judicious use of government funds.

Submit immediately the photocopy of valid ID’s of both the representative claimant and the beneficiary together with the authorization letter, if the claim was not directly made by the beneficiary, to support the release of wages to the foregoing beneficiaries;

Ensure the consistent application of signature of the beneficiaries in the attendance sheets and in the approved DTRs for cash payouts; and 

Provide justification for the payment of the full amount of wage to those beneficiaries with lacking signatures in the attendance sheets.
observations.managementAction:
Liquidation of the fund transfer was already submitted to the source agency.


Signatures were affixed in the attendance sheet and attached DTRs.

observations.recommendation:
We therefore recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Point System to:
Institutionalize the GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data which will provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation;

Undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing gender issues; and

Revisit Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Circular Memorandum No. 2013-01, which identifies the data requirements for particular gender issues.
observations.managementAction: Already established a database provide by the DILG

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to workout in order to come up with a draft of its 10-year solid waste management plan pursuant to the provisions of sections 16 and 17 of RA 9003. Likewise, we recommended that management strictly implement the other relevant provisions of the said law in order to attain a systematic and comprehensive solid waste management for the protection of public health and the environment.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has approved 10-Year SWM Plan

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the former Municipal Accountant to produce the needed liquidation documents and turnover the same to the incumbent Municipal Accountant for submission to the Source Agency.
observations.managementAction: The liquidation report was already submitted in the last quarter of CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE: a. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of properties and equipment and prepare and submit a report thereon; b. Initiate the formulation of plans/strategies for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts to come up with reliable PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the management.
observations.reason:
a. The Municipal Treasurer who could not prioritize the conduct of physical due to bulk of transactions/responsibilities in the Treasurer’s Office.
b. Inventory Committee for the one-time cleansing was not yet created.

observations.recommendation: To correct the deficiency, it was recommended that the LCE: a. Create an Appraisal and Disposal Committee to prepare the IIRUP and initiate the disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 79 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 92-386; b. After the disposal, require the Disposal Committee to submit the necessary documents to the Accountant, as the basis of the latter to drop the unserviceable properties from the book of accounts in accordance with the existing accounting and auditing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by the management.
observations.reason:
a. The Committee was not yet created.
b. Inventory Committee for the one-time cleansing was not yet created.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a. Fast track the implementation of the PPAs funded under the 20% DF consistent with Section 4.6 of COA Circular No. 94-013 and the MOAs for the benefit of the constituents; b. Return to the Source Agency the balance of unutilized fund for completed projects in accordance with COA Circular No. 94-013 to enable the National government to re-appropriate the funds to other priority programs; c. Implement within the timeline as scheduled in the AIP, thru an effective monitoring on the part of the LGU officials, as embodied in the Joint DBM-DILG Memorandum Circular 2017-1.
observations.managementAction: Some prior years PPAs were already implemented. Current year PPAs were partially implemented. The Municipal Engineer stated delays were due to procurement and bidding issues and work extensions caused by bad weather.
observations.reason: Occurrence of failure in the bidding process and delays in preparation/approval of some program of works caused the non-implementation of some PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to install measures that will facilitate the submission of the Contracts and their supporting documents within the prescribed period to facilitate legal, auditorial review, technical evaluation and inspection pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by management to submit perfected contracts within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: There was no document retrieved that proved the contracts for CY 2021 and prior years were perfected and submitted. Legal and auditorial review could not be completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the former Municipal Accountant produce the needed liquidation documents and turnover the same to the incumbent Municipal Accountant for submission to the Source Agency.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the management to produce the needed liquidation documents and turnover the same to the incumbent Municipal Accountant for submission to the Source Agency.
observations.reason: Liquidation documents and other supporting documents were not completed and submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit the lacking documents required in Section 13.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and to settle the suspension in audit and register the Deed of Sale on the subject lot with the Register of Deeds in order to facilitate the issuance of a certificate of title in favor of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by management to produce the required documents and register the deed of sale.
observations.reason: No document of the seller was retrieved that would prove ownership of the subject lot.

observations.recommendation:
1.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) initiate the
formulation of plans/strategies for the one-time cleansing of the PPE
accounts to come up with reliable PPE balances that are verifiable as to
existence, condition and accountability, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-
006.

2.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require:
a. The designated employee and the Inventory Committee members to
prioritize the conduct of physical count of the municipality’s road network
system and submit a report thereon;
b. The Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial
Statements additional necessary information on the agency’s Road
Networks based on the reports that will be prepared and submitted.

3.6 To correct the deficiencies, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive
require the:
a. Municipal Engineer to retrieve all the Certificate of Turn-over and Final
Acceptance, or Deed of Donation or any document that will establish the
transfer and ownership of these assets to the barangay end-user;
b. Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to
reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts both in the LGU and
Barangay books of accounts upon transfer ownership.

4.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary
journal entries to recognize depreciation expense for the three (3) completed
infrastructure projects to correct the net carrying value of the Road
Networks account and the Government Equity account.

5.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the:
a. Municipal Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher for the
following adjustments to record the issuances of various supplies and
materials:
Account Name Debit Credit
Government Equity 1,162,397.61
Agricultural and Marine Supplies for Distribution 437,532.61
Property and Equipment for Distribution 99,200.00
Other Supplies and Materials for Distribution 268,000.00
Other Supplies and Materials for Inventory 357,665.00
1,162,397.61 1,162,397.61
b. Supply Officer to prepare and submit the SSMI for the issued supplies
and materials, and henceforth prepare and submit to the Municipal
Accountant to provide basis for recognizing expenses for inventories;
c. Supply Officer/Inventory Committee to conduct a physical count of all
unused/undistributed inventory items regularly and submit a report
thereon.

6.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Agriculture Officer/Inventory Committee:
a. To conduct inventory of existing Biological Assets and prepare a report
thereon in the prescribed form- Report of the Physical Count of
Biological Assets (RPCBA) providing information on the description and
nature of whether consumable and bearer biological assets, mature or
immature assets non-financial measures or estimates of the physical
quantities of output/produce during the period, existence and carrying
amounts with restrictions, commitments, or risk to facilitate proper
recognition and reporting in accounting records and;
b. To submit the same to the Accounting Office for proper accounting or
reconciliation of records and furnish the same to the COA office.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry to correct the erroneous carrying amount of the Road Network assets due to provision of residual values. Henceforth, require the Municipal Accountant to observe the provisions in COA Circular No. 2015-008 in the computation of depreciation for road network systems.
observations.managementAction: Correcting entries were already made and posted by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the reciprocal accounts and make the necessary adjustments/corrections.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of reciprocal accounts and adjustments were prepared by the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer.
observations.reason: Verification of records and reconciliation of the two accounts and make the necessary adjustments were done by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer, contrary to COA Circular No. 97-002, to immediately conduct an investigation of the noted discrepancies between their respective books of accounts and ensure the conduct of monthly or quarterly reconciliation of records.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer conducted the immediate investigation of the noted discrepancies and coordinated with the respective accountable officers to reconcile records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the immediate renewal of bond with the Bureau of Treasury of all accountable officers whose duties permit/require the possession or custody of funds in compliance to Sec. 101 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: Accountable Officers were required to renew the bond and the Municipal Office accomplished bonding requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Collectors and the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit the monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) and Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable forms (CRAAF) respectively, and adhere to Sec. 37 of COA Circular No. 92-382.
observations.managementAction: The Collectors and the Municipal Treasurer started the preparation of reports. Posting and preparation of reports was done by the employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer: a. advise the Municipal Revenue Collectors to turn-over daily and intact, all collections in accordance with Sections 26 and 33 of COA Circular No. 92-382; b. require the Liquidating Officer to verify the Report of Collections submitted by all accountable officers, count the money turned over to him, sign the certification and receipt portion; c. require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact all collections including the remittances of the Liquidating Officer.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer reminded all Collectors to remit collections on a daily basis. Deposit was made as soon as possible.
observations.reason: No strict compliance. Some collectors still did not comply.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to immediately submit the disbursement vouchers complete with relevant supporting documents and to keenly observe the deadline set in the submission of monthly trial balances and other financial reports.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submits paid disbursement vouchers, but some supporting documents and complete monthly trial balances and financial reports were not submitted on time.
observations.reason: Some monthly trial balances and other financial reports were not submitted on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit the lacking disbursement vouchers and supporting documents. Henceforth, management should adhere strictly with Section 347 of RA 7160 and Section 7.2.1(a) of the RRSA.
observations.managementAction: The lacking DVs were submitted to the Audit Team. Paid some supporting documents were presented by the employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC Secretariat to post the details of the awarded contracts in the GPPB Online Portal, as well as the accumulated reports on utilization of Bayanihan grant in at least three (3) conspicuous public places and submit the said reports or the information on the compliance to posting and reporting requirements to other offices where submission is required.
observations.managementAction: Posting and preparation of reports were done by the employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to require the contractors to post the subject warranty securities.
observations.managementAction: Warranty securities were already required from the contractors.
observations.reason: This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of Suspension or Disallowance in case of failure of the contractors to post the required warranty securities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require: a. The Bids and Awards Committee to ensure that porting of warranty securities be required from the contract awardees in their procurement of goods; b. The Municipal Accountant to withhold retention money or require from the supplier a special bank guarantee, equivalent to at least one percent (1%) of every progress payment to cover the obligation for the warranty; c. The Municipal Accountant and other officials/employees concerned to ensure the completeness of data/information and other supporting documents on the procurement of goods.
observations.managementAction: Warranty securities in the form of retention money were already required from the suppliers of goods procured by the agency. The Municipal Accountant withheld retention money as required.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents on the use of 30% QRF and thus ensure the completeness of supporting documents before payment is made to the suppliers; and require the MSWDO, LDRRMO and other concerned offices to ensure that receipt and distribution of in-kind donations should always be supported with complete documentation for transparency and accountability.
observations.managementAction: The documents were submitted/ presented by management. Some supporting documents were retrieved by the concerned offices.
observations.reason: The retrieval of documents was not completed by the concerned offices.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require: a. all drivers of motor vehicles to prepare and submit the requisition of fuel or oil products; b. the Supply Officer to require from each requisitioner of fuel and oil products an approved Requisition and Issuance Slip (RIS) and Driver’s Trip Ticket; c. the Supply Officer/personnel in-charge to serially number the Driver’s Trip Tickets issued, prepare Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for each motor vehicle and submit on a monthly basis the said report to the auditor for audit/verification.
observations.managementAction: The Supply Officer prepared the required RIS and Driver’s Trip Ticket and the reports. The Purchase Requests were prepared. Some documents were already required for procurement of products.
observations.reason: No strict implementation of policies. Some documents required for procurement were not provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to workout and come up with a draft of its 10-year solid waste management plan pursuant to the provisions of sections 16 and 17 of RA 9003, and to conduct a workshop/training or hire consultancy services for the formulation of the SWMP.
observations.managementAction: Management considered and conducted some of the workshop/training and hired consultancy services. Some outputs were presented by the employees.
observations.reason: No strict implementation of specific plans and procedures. Some activities were not yet completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly observe the provisions of Section 12(c)(7) of RA 10121 and NDRRMC/DBM/DILG JMC Nos. 2014-1 and 2012-02. The MDRRMO should be required to submit budgetary proposal for the regular operation of the MDRRM Office so that the 5% calamity fund should be exclusively appropriated and utilized for expenses directly related to disaster risk-reduction management.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRM Council stopped the charging of administrative expenses to DRRM funds. The MDRRMO was required to submit budgetary proposal.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require: a) the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the former OIC Municipal Accountant to retrieve previous records in order to identify the person/s accountable; b) the former OIC Accountant to turnover all documents and records that will show the details of the undeposited collections including the name/s of person/s accountable, otherwise, institute appropriate disciplinary action against her for allowing the funds in the hands of the persons accountable without the proper recording/reporting; c) the person/s identified as accountable to immediately deposit the amount to the LGU’s depository bank.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant coordinated with the former OIC Accountant. The Municipal Accountant retrieved some documents and turned over documents to the incumbent Municipal Accountant for submission to the Source Agency.
observations.reason: No document could be retrieved and provided to the auditor. Some documents were not completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management comply with the General Provisions on salary deductions and ensure that net take home pay is not lower than the required minimum amount.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant did not allow deduction of loans which would reduce the employee’s monthly net take home pay of the required minimum amount. The concerned employees were advised accordingly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. the Municipal Mayor require the identified Persons Liable in the Notice of Suspension or disallowance to demand by the liable persons to settle their audit suspensions; b. the Municipal Accountant send copy of the Notices to the Persons Liable for their information on their unsettled suspensions/disallowance.
observations.managementAction: Some Notices of Disallowance were already settled. The Municipal Accountant sent copies of the Notices to the Persons Liable for their information.
observations.reason: Some Notices were already settled. Other suspensions and disallowances are not yet settled due to the failure to retrieve documents and non-compliance by the persons liable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a) Fast track the implementation of the PPAs consistent with Section 4.6 of COA Circular No. 94-013 and the MOAs for the benefit of the constituents; b) Return to the Source Agency the balance of unutilized fund for completed projects in accordance with COA Circular No. 94-013; c) Ensure the implementation of projects funded by other source agencies and coordinate with the source agencies for the turnover of funds or return of unutilized amounts.
observations.managementAction: Some projects funded by other source agencies were already implemented and some funds were returned to SAs. Management implemented some PPAs and coordinated with source agencies.
observations.reason: Some PPAs funded by other source agencies were implemented and unutilized balances were returned to the source agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a) properly plan, prioritize and evaluate the programs, projects and activities to be funded out of the available LDRRM fund; b) submit the Report on Utilization of DRRM Funds, including the utilization of the Special Trust Fund, on a monthly basis using the prescribed format; c) ensure the optimum use of the fund by implementing the identified priority PPAs to attain the objective and purpose of the establishment of the DRRM funds.
observations.managementAction: Only some of the prior years and current year PPAs were implemented. The LDRRM Office submitted electronic copy of Utilization Report. Some PPAs were implemented during the year.
observations.reason: Electronic copy of Utilization Report was submitted. Some PPAs under the thematic areas and the available appropriation for the year were implemented; except some PPAs were implemented during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the GAD focal person to ensure the utilization of GAD funds through the implementation of programs and projects addressing issues on women empowerment and gender equality so that the intended beneficiaries are assured of the services, protection, and other assistance that may be provided.
observations.managementAction: Most PPAs included in the GAD Plan and Budget were implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) demand the Municipal Accountant to liquidate all her outstanding cash advances for Operating Expenses; otherwise, stop granting other form of cash advance unless all her cash advances are first settled; b) require the Municipal Accountant to apply the required action stated in Sec. 3.3.2(b) of COA Circular No 96-004 to all officers and employees who did not responded accordingly to the demand letters issued; c) remind the Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of granting cash advances, if the previous cash advance is not yet settled.
observations.managementAction: The demand was implemented. The Municipal Accountant and other concerned offices implemented collection and liquidation processes. The allowance for doubtful accounts was set up for the uncollected amounts.
observations.reason: Some cash advances were liquidated and collections were made. Some prior year amounts remained uncollected due to lack of strict monitoring and implementation of sanctions in case of noncompliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management strictly comply with the documentation, accreditation, awarding and monitoring procedures and submit the lacking documentation for the utilization/liquidation of funds. Also, direct the Municipal Accountant to draw a journal entry voucher to reclassify fund transfers to the appropriate asset account - Due from NGOs/POs, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2004-008 dated September 20, 2004.
observations.managementAction: An allowance for impairment was set up by management. Some documents were retrieved and submitted.
observations.reason: The supporting documents for the utilization/liquidation of the funds could not be retrieved and the amounts remained uncollected. An allowance for impairment was set up to recognize doubtful accounts.

observations.recommendation:
5.18 We recommended that the LCE:
a) Require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of
properties and equipment and prepare and submit a report thereon;
b) Initiate the formulation of plans/strategies for the one-time cleansing of
the PPE accounts to come up with reliable PPE balances that are
verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability, pursuant to COA
Circular No. 2020-006.

2.6 We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to
prepare an adjusting entry to correct the erroneous carrying amount of the
Road Network assets due to provision of residual values. Henceforth,
require the Municipal Accountant to observe the provisions in COA Circular
No. 2015-008 in the computation of depreciation for road network systems.

3.10 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to prepare reconciliation between the two accounts and make the
necessary adjusting entry to ensure that movements made on the receivable
account in the transferring fund have corresponding movements on the
payable account in the receiving fund. We likewise recommended that the
Municipal Accountant ensure the transfer of interest accruing from the
Trust Fund bank accounts to the General Fund at the end of the year so that
the amount is recognized in the Statement of Financial Performance and be
made available for annual budget preparation.

4.11 We recommended that the LCE:
a. Create an Appraisal and Disposal Committee to prepare the IIRUP and
initiate the disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with
Section 79 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 92-386;
b. After the disposal, require the Disposal Committee to submit the
necessary documents to the Accountant, as the basis of the latter to drop
the unserviceable properties from the book of accounts in accordance
with the existing accounting and auditing rules and regulations.

5.5 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to conduct an immediate
investigation of the noted discrepancies between their respective books of
accounts. Henceforth, require the Accountable Officers to strictly comply
with the provision in COA Circular 97-002 on the reconciliation of records
on a monthly or quarterly basis, to ascertain the balances of accountabilities
of the Municipal Treasurer at the end of each accounting period.

6.4 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the immediate
renewal of bond with the Bureau of Treasury of all accountable officers
whose duties permit/require the possession or custody of funds in compliance
to Sec. 101 of PD 1445.

7.3 We recommended that the LCE require the Collectors and the Municipal
Treasurer to prepare the monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable
Forms (RAAF) and Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable
forms (CRAAF) respectively, pursuant to Sec. 37 of COA Circular No. 92-
382 in order to facilitate effective monitoring and control of the Accountable
Forms of the LGU.

8.9 We recommended that the LCE:
a. Advise the Municipal Treasurer to strictly require all the Revenue
Collectors to turn-over daily and intact, all collections in accordance with
Sections 26 and 33 of COA Circular No. 92-382;
b. Require the Liquidating Officer to verify the Report of Collections
submitted by all accountable officers, count the money turned over to
him, sign the certification and receipt portion;
c. Require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact all collections
including the remittances of the Liquidating Officer.

9.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to submit
immediately the lacking disbursement vouchers complete with relevant
supporting documents and to keenly observe the deadline set in the
submission of monthly trial balances and other financial reports.

10.5 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Accountant and
Treasurer to immediately submit the lacking disbursement vouchers and
supporting documents. Henceforth, management should adhere strictly with
the pertinent provisions of Section 347 of RA 7160 and Section 7.2.1 (a) of the
RRSA.

11.8 We recommended that the LCE require the BAC Secretariat to post the details
of the awarded contracts in the GPPB Online Portal, as well as the
accumulated reports on utilization of Bayanihan grant in at least three (3)
conspicuous public places and submit the said reports or the information on
the compliance to posting and reporting requirements to other offices where
submission is required. Henceforth, ensure that the guidelines on the posting
and reporting requirements be strictly followed in all financial transactions
and program/activity implementation of the LGU, for the succeeding funds
received from NGAs and other source organizations/agencies.

12.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to
require the contractors concerned to post the subject warranty securities.
This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of
Suspension or Disallowance in case of failure of the contractors to post the
required warranty securities.

12.9 Henceforth, the Municipality should strictly enforce compliance with the
pertinent provisions of RA No. 9184 and its RIRR, and Section 9.1.1.4 of
COA Circular No. 2012-001 in the implementation of infrastructure projects,
particularly on the posting of warranty securities before final payment.

13.9 We recommended that the LCE require:
a. The Bids and Awards Committee to ensure that porting of warranty
securities be required from the contract awardees in their procurement of
goods;
b. The Municipal Accountant to withhold retention money or require from
the supplier a special bank guarantee, equivalent to at least one percent
(1%) of every progress payment to cover the obligation for the warranty;
c. The Municipal Accountant and other officials/employees concerned to
ensure the completeness of data/information and other supporting
documents on the procurement of goods.

14.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents on the
use of 30% QRF and thus ensure the completeness of supporting
documents before payment is made to suppliers;
b. Require the MSWDO, LDRRMO and other concerned offices to ensure
that receipt and distribution of in-kind donations should always be
supported with complete documentation for transparency and
accountability.

15.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
a.) Require all drivers of motor vehicles to prepare and submit the Monthly
Report of Official Travels for each motor vehicle;
b.) Instruct the Supply Officer to require from each requisitioner of fuel and oil
products an approved Requisition and Issuance Slip (RIS) and Driver’s Trip
Ticket;
c.) Direct the Supply Officer/personnel in-charge to serially number the
Driver’s Trip Tickets issued, prepare Monthly Report of Fuel
Consumption for each motor vehicle and submit on a monthly basis the
said report to the auditor for audit/verification.

16.7 We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Solid Waste
Management Board to workout in order to come up with a draft of its 10-
year solid waste management plan pursuant to the provisions of sections 16
and 17 of RA 9003. Likewise, we recommend that management strictly
implement the other relevant provisions of the said law in order to attain a
systematic and comprehensive solid waste management for the protection of
public health and the environment.

17.11 We recommended that management strictly observe the provisions of Section
12 Paragraph (c) of Republic Act No. 10121 and NDRRMC/DBM/DILG
JMC Nos. 2014-1 and 2012-02. The MDRRMO should be required to submit
budgetary proposal for the regular operation of the MDRRM Office so that
the 5% calamity fund should be exclusively appropriated and utilized for
expenses directly related to disaster risk-reduction management.

18.11 We recommended that management implement immediately the PPAs
funded under the 20% DF of the current and prior years. Henceforth
implement within the timeline as scheduled in the AIP, thru an effetive
monitoring on the part of the MPDC, consistent with the mandated
responsibility of other LGU officials, as embodied in the Joint DBM-DILG
Memorandum Circular 2017-1.

We recommended that management:
Assess these types of receivables and provide Allowance for Impairment corresponding to the unliquidated amounts pursuant to Paragraph 67 of IPSAS 29 to reduce the affected receivables accounts to its appropriate carrying amounts.
Send demand letters to the DepEd employees through the Regional Director of the said agency.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Treasurer and Assessor to strictly comply with the provisions of Section 20 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs. A duly certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year should be prepared and submitted to the Municipal Accountant which should serve as the basis for recording the Real Property Tax Receivables/Special Education Tax Receivables at the beginning of the year.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to:
Make the necessary adjustment to present the current portion of the Loans Payable/Financial Liabilities account of P1,888,665.84.
Determine yearly the maturing/current Loans Payable and present the amount under Current Liabilities section in the Statement of Financial Position as of the reporting period.

We recommended that the LCE require the Accountant to:
Prepare adjusting journal entry to transfer the amount of P2,361,036.60 from the General Fund to the Trust Fund to correct the balance of Trust Liability-LDRRM account.
Henceforth, transfer the yearly unutilized balance of LDRRM funds at the end of the year to reflect the correct balance of the Trust Liability account at every accounting period.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to install measures that will facilitate the submission of the Contracts and their supporting documents within the prescribed period to facilitate legal, auditorial review, technical evaluation and inspection pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Bids and Awards Committee through its Chairman and other officials to strictly follow the provisions of RA 9184 and its IRR especially on the provisions of Liquidated Damages in the Contracts, and on the documentary requirements. We likewise recommended to submit the lacking documents, or a Notice of Suspension shall be issued to demand submission.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Assessor to take appropriate steps in coordination with the Provincial Assessor so that the CY 2013 General Revision of Assessments and Property Classification be updated accordingly to enable management to collect higher amount of Real Property and Special Education taxes.

We recommended that LCE advise the Municipal Treasurer to strictly require all the Revenue Collectors and the liquidating officer to turn-over daily and intact, all collections in accordance with Sections 26 and 33 of COA Circular No. 92-382. Moreover, require the Municipal Treasurer to verify the Report of Collections submitted by all accountable officers, count the money turned over to him, sign the certification and receipt portion and deposit intact all collections including the remittances of the Liquidating Officer.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the responsible officials/employees to remit immediately the withheld funds to PHIC to avoid incurrence of penalties.
This is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Disallowance to the designated remitting officer for the penalties that were paid by the Municipality due to their failure to remit on time the contributions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the responsible officials/employees to immediately comply with the requirements set by Pag-IBIG and ensure a timely remittance of loan repayments and contributions pursuant to RA 9679.

We recommended that LCE require the Municipal Accountant to comply with the General Provisions of the General Appropriations Act, particularly on the rules and regulations on employee’s monthly net take home pay in the preparation of the payrolls, that the same shall in no case be lower than the required minimum amount specified therein.

We recommended that the LCE require the former Municipal Accountant to produce the needed liquidation documents and turnover the same to the incumbent Municipal Accountant for submission to the Source Agency.

We recommended that:
a.	the Municipal Mayor require the identified Persons Liable in the Notice of Suspension or the Notice of Disallowance to settle their audit suspension or disallowance.
b.	the Municipal Accountant send copy of the Notices to the Persons Liable for their information on their unsettled suspensions/disallowance.

advances.
2. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to send Notices and/or Demand
Letters to all municipal officials and employees to liquidate their cash
advances (Salary Deduction) or else apply the required monitoring action
stated in Sec. 3.3.2b of COA Circular No 96-004.
3. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to send Notices and/or Demand
Letters to employees of other government agencies through their
respective agency head for the settlement of their cash advances.
4. Remind the Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of granting cash
advances, if the previous cash advance is not yet settled.

PPAs consistent with the MOAs for the immediate use of the intended
beneficiaries; (b) return to the Source Agency the balance of unutilized fund for
projects already completed in accordance with COA Circular No. 94-013.

development and environmental management outcomes and optimum benefits
for the constituents.

connected with the LGU having an administrative supervision of the
Accountable Officer for internal control measure, instead of somebody
from the school.

procurement planning in accordance with RA 9184 that will regulate the
procurement activities and discourage reimbursement of expenses.

This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of
Suspension/Disallowance in audit.

observations.managementAction: the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant are now complying with the submission of the lacking documents.
observations.reason: Related discussion is reiterated in #4, Part II of this report, which recommends the submission of the lacking documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management use the prescribed AF 51 in the issuance of receipts for all the collections of the agency. In instances where issuance of AF 51 is impractical like payment of EUF which costs P50.00 and would require speedy issuance, pre-numbered cash tickets signed at the back by a duly authorized official indicating its authenticity may be used. Moreover, the rules and regulations over the handling of collections, deposits, accountable forms and reporting thereof should be strictly observed.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Treasurer already implemented the issuance of AF 51 intact.
observations.reason: Collections were still not deposited.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: 1) Comply strictly with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the granting, utilization, and liquidation of cash advances; 2) Require the accountable officers to liquidate the overdue cash advances. Invoke the provisions of law authorizing the over withholding of salary of personnel who unjustifiably fails to settle his cash advances within the prescribed period; and File appropriate legal action, if warranted to those who have failed to liquidate the overdue cash advances after exhausting appropriate means including those who are no longer in the government service.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant informed the Team that she had already issued demand letters to concerned official and employees to liquidate the overdue cash advances.
observations.reason: AAR already issued demand letters to concerned official and employees. Balance of Advances for Officers and Employees in the PPSAS Combined Statement of Financial Position showed a remarkable decrease of 237.74% over last year's balance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to record the purchases as Inventories in accordance with PPSAS 12. observations.managementAction: Management has already submitted the LDRRMF Investment Plan and Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management create and strengthen the GAD Focal Point System, establish the GAD data base, prepare and submit the GAD Plan and the Accomplishment Report, in accordance with PCW-DILG-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management create the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board that will prepare and strictly implement the Solid Waste Management Plan and Budget for the safe and sanitary management of solid waste generated in areas under LGUs jurisdiction pursuant to Section 12 of Republic Act No. 9003.

observations.managementAction: Management has already submitted the list of all on-going projects/programs/activities (“PPA”) for CYs 2015 and 2016.
